AdminSettings.offerTimeoutHint: How long a driver has to accept an offered parcel before it moves to the next one. 0 = assign directly with no accept step.
AdminSettings.offerRoundsHint: How many drivers to try before alerting dispatch to assign manually.
AdminSettings.jobBoardHint: Post unassigned Express parcels on a board where any eligible driver can see the details and claim the job — first tap wins.
AdminSettings.jobBoardWindowHint: How long a parcel stays board-only before auto-offers also start chasing a driver. 0 = start auto-offers immediately; the parcel stays claimable either way.
AdminSettings.jobBoardMaxJobsHint: A driver already carrying this many active deliveries can't claim more from the board. 0 = no cap.
AdminSettings.err_badBoardWindow: The board window must be between 0 and 1440 minutes.
AdminSettings.err_badBoardCap: The active-jobs cap must be between 0 and 100.
AdminSettings.boardReminderHint: While jobs sit unclaimed on the board, re-ping eligible drivers once per this many minutes (one combined notification). 0 = off.
AdminSettings.err_badBoardReminder: The board reminder must be between 0 and 1440 minutes.
AdminSettings.pickupDeadlineHint: A driver who accepted or claimed a job but hasn't scanned the parcel within this many hours loses it automatically — it's re-dispatched to other drivers. 0 = off.
AdminSettings.err_badPickupDeadline: The pickup deadline must be between 0 and 168 hours.
AdminSettings.dispatchHoursHint: Yemen local hour, 0–23. Outside this window parcels queue and offer clocks pause; equal values = 24/7 dispatch.
AdminSettings.err_badOfferTimeout: The offer window must be between 0 and 1440 minutes.
AdminSettings.err_badOfferRounds: Offer rounds must be between 1 and 20.
AdminSettings.err_badDispatchHours: Dispatch hours must be whole hours between 0 and 23.
AdminSettings.sellerShipDaysHint: Unshipped orders auto-cancel (paid buyers refunded) after this many days; the seller is warned a day before. 0 = off.
AdminSettings.minAcceptRateHint: Drivers whose 90-day offer acceptance falls below this stop getting auto-offers. 0 = off; manual dispatch always works.
AdminSettings.minAcceptOffersHint: Answered offers a driver needs before the acceptance gate applies to them.
AdminSettings.err_badAcceptRate: Acceptance rate must be 0–100% with a sample size of 1–1000 offers.
AdminSettings.walletBillsHint: Buyers pay bills / top up airtime from their wallet; admins fulfill each purchase manually until a provider API is wired.
AdminSettings.walletOutflowHint: Max that can leave a wallet (send + cash-out + bills) per rolling window. 0 = no limit; VERIFIED users get 5×.
AdminSettings.pointFeeHint: Paid to a delivery point for each delivered parcel routed through it.
AdminSettings.maxAttemptsHint: Failed attempts before a parcel should be returned to the seller.
AdminSettings.pointsEnabledHint: Let sellers drop Hezalli Express parcels at partner points.
AdminSettings.err_badMaxAttempts: Max attempts must be between 1 and 10.
AdminSettings.pickupFeeHint: What buyers pay to collect from a Hezalli Point themselves. 0 = free.

AdminAssistant.title: Shadi — AI assistant
AdminAssistant.desc: Everything about شادي: identity, credentials, channels, voice, and cost limits.
AdminAssistant.enabledHint: Master switch — hides the chat bubble and pauses the chat API and messaging channels.
AdminAssistant.avatarHint: Shown on the chat bubble and inside the chat window. Square images look best.
AdminAssistant.personaPlaceholder: e.g. You are warm and a little playful. Speak Yemeni Arabic dialect. Always suggest 2–3 relevant products. Never promise delivery dates. Recommend Hezalli Express for urgent orders.

AdminAssistant.greetingPlaceholder: e.g. Hi! I'm Shadi 👋 How can I help you shop today?
AdminAssistant.greetingHint: The first line shown when someone opens the chat. Empty = the built-in greeting for each language.
AdminAssistant.temperatureHint: Lower = focused and factual; higher = more varied and creative.
AdminAssistant.maxTokensHint: Longest reply Shadi can give, in tokens (~4 characters each). 128–8192. Default 1024.
AdminAssistant.keyHint: Create a free key at Google AI Studio (aistudio.google.com). Stored in the database and never shown again; the GEMINI_API_KEY server variable is the fallback.
AdminAssistant.keyStatusDb: A key is saved here — Shadi is using it.
AdminAssistant.keyStatusEnv: No key saved here — Shadi is using the server's GEMINI_API_KEY variable.
AdminAssistant.keyStatusNone: No key configured — Shadi stays hidden until you add one.
AdminAssistant.modelHint: Gemini model id, e.g. gemini-2.5-flash (fast & cheap) or gemini-2.5-pro (smarter). Empty = server default.
AdminAssistant.channelsDesc: The website chat bubble always follows the master switch above. Messaging channels can be switched individually.
AdminAssistant.channelsHint: "Not connected" means the channel's credentials (bot token / access token) aren't set — the toggle only matters once they are.
AdminAssistant.tgTokenHint: Paste the token BotFather gave you (message @BotFather → /newbot). Connecting verifies the token and registers the webhook automatically.
AdminAssistant.err_badTokenFormat: That doesn't look like a Telegram bot token (expected 123456789:AA…).

AdminAssistant.err_webhookFailed: Telegram refused the webhook registration. Check the site is reachable over HTTPS and try again.
AdminAssistant.replyModeHint: "Match" answers voice notes with voice and text with text. Voice notes are always understood regardless.
AdminAssistant.voiceHint: Gemini text-to-speech voice used for voice notes.
AdminAssistant.stylePlaceholder: e.g. Say this warmly and naturally, like a friendly Yemeni shop assistant:

How.b_subtitle: Order in minutes, pay only when your parcel is in your hands — here is the whole journey.
How.b_s1d: Search thousands of products from local sellers, add to cart, and check out in a minute.
How.b_s2d: Cash on delivery (pay nothing now), a transfer via Jawali/Jaib/Kuraimi/bank, or instantly from your HezalliPay balance.
How.b_s3d: Sellers ship with Hezalli Express drivers or through partner pickup points — you can follow every step live.
How.b_s4d: While your parcel is on the way, your order page shows a personal QR code — it proves the parcel reaches YOU, nobody else.
How.b_s5t: At the door: cash or one tap
How.b_s5d: Hand the driver cash — or tap “Pay from wallet” before he arrives and hand over nothing at all.
How.b_s6d: Confirm you got it. If something is wrong, the return window and dispute team have your back.
How.b_g1d: You pay only when the parcel is in front of you. No parcel, no payment — ever.
How.b_g2t: Skip the cash at the door
How.b_g2d: Keep a HezalliPay balance and settle a COD order with one tap while the driver is on the way.
How.b_g3d: Prefer collecting yourself? Choose a nearby Hezalli Point at checkout and pick it up when it suits you.
How.b_g4d: Your delivery code means the driver can prove the right person received the parcel.
How.b_g5d: Refunds land in your wallet instantly, cashback collects there, and you can pay orders and bills from it.
How.b_g6d: A clear return window, a required seller response time, and a dispute path if you disagree.
How.d_subtitle: Your day, your cash limit, and how your earnings grow — in one picture.
How.d_s1d: New deliveries are assigned to you automatically — you get a notification and the job appears in your list.
How.d_s2d: Collect it from the seller or scan your collection QR at a Hezalli Point counter.
How.d_s3d: Hand it to the buyer — add their name, a photo, or scan their delivery code as proof.
How.d_s4d: For COD you collect the amount shown on the job. If it says PAID digitally, the buyer already paid from their wallet — collect nothing.
How.d_s5t: Hand the cash in the same day
How.d_s5d: Pay it at any Hezalli Point, the office, or send it by Jawali/Jaib/Kuraimi transfer and submit the reference.
How.d_s6d: Every delivery adds your fee to earnings. Sweep them to your HezalliPay wallet any time — as long as you hold no cash.
How.d_fCaption: Deposit = money you left with Hezalli. Wallet pledge = balance you locked from your own wallet. Trust bonus = every 20 clean deliveries add $10, up to $100. The bigger your limit, the more COD jobs you can carry at once.
How.d_bOkD: Your cash is under the limit and fresh. Jobs keep coming.
How.d_bWarnD: You are near your limit. Pay at a point soon to keep receiving jobs.
How.d_bStopD: Over the limit, or cash held more than 24 hours. Hand it in and jobs resume immediately.
How.d_g1d: Any COD cash must be handed in within a day — even small amounts. Old cash pauses new jobs.
How.d_g2d: No need to reach the office: any Hezalli Point counter can take your cash and it settles instantly.
How.d_g3d: Send the cash by Jawali, Jaib, Floosak, Kuraimi or bank, submit the reference on your ledger page, and staff confirm it.
How.d_g4d: While you hold cash, your earnings wait. Hand the cash in — or it can be settled from your earnings.
How.d_g5d: Deliver well and your limit rises automatically. A deposit or wallet pledge raises it instantly.
How.d_g6d: Every dollar in and out is on your ledger and monthly statement — check it any time.
How.m_title: How the delivery money cycle works
How.m_subtitle: Where the cash is at every moment — and the tools you use to keep it moving.
How.m_s1d: On a COD delivery, the driver (or a point counter) takes the buyer's cash. It lands on that person's ledger the same second.
How.m_s2d: Drivers and points each have a cash limit and a 24-hour clock. Over either one, they stop receiving new work automatically.
How.m_s3d: Cash goes to a point counter or the office — or they transfer it over a rail and file a claim with the reference.
How.m_s4d: Check the transfer really arrived in the Remittances queue. Approve to settle their ledger; reject with a reason if not.
How.m_s5d: Cash-on-hand drops to zero, blocks lift automatically, and everything stays on the audit trail.
How.m_g1d: Every parcel on the road, reassignment, and stuck-shipment alerts.
How.m_g2d: Each driver's cash, earnings, deposit, and limit breakdown. Record hand-ins, pay earnings, or settle cash from earnings in one click.
How.m_g3d: Each hub's parcels, cash side, deposit, and payout requests — with net settlement: held cash is withheld from what you pay them.
How.m_g4d: Pending transfer claims from drivers and points. Approve settles the ledger atomically; double-approve is impossible.
How.m_g5d: Record security deposits — each dollar raises that person's cash limit by exactly one dollar.
How.m_g6d: Approve new drivers and points, group couriers into fleet partners.
How.m_bOkD: Cash under limits and fresh — nothing to do.
How.m_bWarnD: Near a limit or parcels going stale — worth a look today.
How.m_bStopD: Blocked driver or over-limit point — the system already stopped their flow; chase the cash.
How.m_footer: Platform rules — the limits, timers, and trust bonus — are set by the admin in Settings. You work the ledgers; the rules protect you both.
How.w_subtitle: Money in, money out, and the desks that keep every wallet honest.
How.w_i1t: User sends money on a rail
How.w_i1d: Jawali, Jaib, Floosak, Kuraimi, bank or USDT — then files a top-up request with the reference.
How.w_i2d: Check the rail account. The request carries the amount, method, and reference to compare.
How.w_i3d: Approve and the balance appears instantly as an immutable ledger entry. Reject returns nothing — no money moved.
How.w_o1d: Only VERIFIED users with a saved payout destination can cash out. Review identities in the KYC queue.
How.w_o2d: The amount is debited the moment they ask, so it can never be spent twice while waiting.
How.w_o3d: Send the money on the rail, mark PAID with the reference. Reject returns the funds automatically.
How.w_g1d: Manual prepaid order payments and top-ups awaiting confirmation, in one queue.
How.w_g2d: Reserved cash-out requests to pay or reject — every decision notifies the user.
How.w_g3d: Sellers, points, and driver earnings leave through payout queues with references.
How.w_g4d: Approve or reject identity documents; verified users get higher wallet limits.
How.w_g5d: Total liability vs ledger sums across all wallets — one page proves the books balance.
How.w_g6d: P2P sends and QR payments with velocity caps enforced automatically.
How.a_title: How Hezalli works, end to end
How.a_subtitle: From a buyer's tap to money in a seller's pocket — and every protection in between.
How.a_s1d: Buyer checks out with COD, a manual rail transfer, or their HezalliPay balance.
How.a_s2d: Wallet pays instantly. Rail transfers wait for staff confirmation. COD stays unpaid until delivery — unless the buyer pays from their wallet at the door.
How.a_s3d: Sellers ship direct, via Hezalli Express drivers, or through partner Points — every handover is a QR scan with one accountable party at all times.
How.a_s4d: Proof captured, COD cash lands on the collector's ledger, fees accrue to driver and point in the same transaction.
How.a_s5d: Limits, the 24h rule, and remittance claims pull every dollar back — nobody can hold more than they'd lose by keeping it.
How.a_s6d: Sales settle to seller balances (escrow), leave via payout requests or sweep into HezalliPay wallets.
How.a_g1d: Cash held too long or over the limit pauses new assignments automatically. Setting: COD max age, driver cash limit.
How.a_g2d: Security deposits from drivers and points raise their limits 1:1 — recorded by staff, audited.
How.a_g3d: Drivers can lock their own HezalliPay balance as collateral. Pledged money cannot leave the wallet.
How.a_g4d: Clean delivery history grows a driver's limit automatically — settings control the step and cap.
How.a_g5d: Drivers and points remit by rail transfer + reference; staff verify before anything settles.
How.a_g6d: Buyers can settle COD from their wallet before handover — the driver never touches cash. Toggle in Settings.
How.a_r1d: Dispatch, drivers, points, deposits, remittances — all day-to-day delivery money.
How.a_r2d: Top-ups, withdrawals, KYC, payouts, reconciliation — the HezalliPay domain.
How.a_r3d: Partner shop counters and fleet companies extend your network without staff.
How.a_r4d: Set the rules: limits, timers, fees, toggles. Staff work the ledgers; only you change the game.
How.s_subtitle: From your first listing to money you can spend — step by step.
How.s_s1d: Add products with photos, variants, and stock. They go live the moment they're approved.
How.s_s2d: You're notified instantly. The buyer may pay cash on delivery, by rail transfer, or from their HezalliPay wallet.
How.s_s3d: Hand it to a Hezalli Express driver, drop it at a nearby Hezalli Point, or use your own carrier — with live tracking either way.
How.s_s4d: The buyer receives with proof. COD cash is Hezalli's job to collect — never yours to chase.
How.s_s5d: The sale (minus commission) settles to your balance automatically on delivery.
How.s_s6d: Request a payout to your bank or rail — or sweep the balance into your HezalliPay wallet and use it instantly.
How.s_g1d: Drop parcels at a partner counter near you — no waiting for a driver. Two-hop routing reaches other cities.
How.s_g2d: Platform drivers with QR chain-of-custody: someone is accountable for your parcel at every moment.
How.s_g3d: Hezalli collects the cash, guards it with limits and deposits, and settles your sale either way.
How.s_g4d: Keep earnings in your balance for payouts, or move them to your wallet to spend on the spot.
How.s_g5d: A clear window, a response timer, and a dispute path — so problems end with a decision, not a fight.
How.s_g6d: Coupons, flash sales, and store vouchers to push your best products.
How.p_subtitle: Your shop is a parcel hub: every handover is a scan, and every scan is money accounted for.
How.p_flowTitle: A parcel's day at your counter
How.p_s1d: Scan the parcel label in Receive mode — custody moves to you, and a shelf code keeps it findable.
How.p_s2d: Scan the driver's QR to see their pickup list, then hand over the whole manifest in one tap.
How.p_s3d: For counter orders, scan the buyer's code, collect the COD amount shown, and the parcel is delivered.
How.p_s4d: Scan returned parcels back in — the buyer is notified and can pick a redelivery day.
How.p_s5t: Out of attempts → return to seller
How.p_s5d: After the attempt limit, one scan returns the parcel to the seller — refunds and stock resolve automatically.
How.p_s6d: Every delivered parcel credits your handling fee. Sweep earnings to your wallet or request a payout.
How.p_fCaption: COD cash you hold for Hezalli counts against this limit. Over it, new routing and driver cash-ins pause until you remit.
How.p_bOkD: Cash well under the limit — parcels flow normally.
How.p_bWarnD: Over 80% of your limit — remit soon to avoid a pause.
How.p_bStopD: Over the limit — no new routing or driver cash-ins until you remit.
How.p_g1d: Type a shelf like A3 at the receive scan — the manifest and pickup screens lead with it.
How.p_g2d: Two-hop parcels enter the network at your hub on their way to another city — you earn a transfer fee on delivery.
How.p_g3d: A parcel that hasn't moved for days gets an aged badge and a reminder — keep them moving.
How.p_g4d: Take a driver's COD cash at your counter — both ledgers update in one atomic step.
How.p_g5d: Send held cash over Jawali, Jaib, Floosak, Kuraimi, or bank and file the reference for approval.
How.p_g6d: Opening balance, the month's activity, closing balance — for earnings and cash, with a CSV export.

Express.title: Hezalli Express
Express.metaTitle: Hezalli Express — how a parcel moves
Express.heroTitle: How a parcel moves, checkout to delivered
Express.heroSubtitle: Hezalli Express is the marketplace's own fast-delivery arm — now a complete last-mile network. The buyer pays for speed at checkout, the seller hands the parcel to Hezalli directly or through a partner point, and one of our own couriers delivers it — every handover proven with a QR scan.
Express.tiersSubtitle: The buyer picks a shipping tier per seller in the cart. The fee is always recalculated server-side — a tampered client price never sticks.
Express.t1Desc: Normal shipping. A store's "free over $X" offer waives this tier only.
Express.t2Desc: A separate fee — the seller's per-zone price, or the platform default. Always charged, never waived.
Express.t3Desc: The buyer collects their own parcel from a partner shop. Shown only when an active point exists; unavailable for freight.
Express.shotCheckout: The real tier-selection screen — Express selected
Express.s1d: Sees the fee and delivery estimate, chooses Express for that seller, and checks out. Freight items require a delivery window.
Express.s2t: Seller ships direct or via a Hezalli Point
Express.s2d: Hits "Ship" — the carrier defaults to Hezalli Express — and a label prints with a QR code and barcode. The parcel can go straight to a courier, or drop at a nearby point.
Express.shotShip: The seller's ship panel — carrier defaults to Hezalli Express
Express.shotLabel: The printed shipping label — QR and barcode for every handover
Express.s3t: The platform offers it to a courier — automatically
Express.s3d: The moment it ships, the best-fit courier (by balance or true distance) gets an offer with a time limit to accept or decline. With the job board on, any eligible courier can claim it themselves.
Express.shotOffers: Delivery offers in the driver app — accept or decline
Express.s4t: The courier scans, delivers, and captures proof
Express.s4d: Advances through Picked up → Out for delivery → Delivered, collects cash on delivery, or logs a failed attempt with a reason.
Express.shotJob: The job screen — COD collection, one-tap call, and status buttons
Express.shotProof: Delivery confirmation — recipient name, delivery code, and a doorstep photo
Express.s5t: When it stalls: reschedule or return
Express.s5d: A failed parcel stays retriable and the buyer can request redelivery. After the attempt limit, it auto-returns to the seller with a refund or cancellation.
Express.s6d: Delivery starts an auto-complete countdown. The buyer confirms receipt, the seller is settled, the courier's fee accrues, and the buyer rates the courier.
Express.shotTracking: The public tracking page — full timeline and a QR to reopen it
Express.dispatchTitle: How a parcel finds a courier
Express.dispatchSubtitle: Two channels working together: offers pushed to a chosen courier, and an open board couriers pull from themselves — both under the same gates.
Express.shotDispatch: The admin dispatch board — active parcels and bulk assignment
Express.d1d: The chosen courier gets a notification and a deadline to answer — a tap, or implicitly their first scan. Decline or timeout cascades to the next courier, excluding anyone already asked.
Express.d2d: When enabled, unassigned parcels are posted for every eligible courier to browse: destination, size, COD amount, and their own delivery fee. First tap wins.
Express.d3d: An operator-set strategy: balanced (fewest active jobs) or nearest (true distance, then destination governorate, then least loaded).
Express.d4d: Each vehicle has weight, volume, and parcel-count limits. Freight is direct-only and needs an appointment; parcels headed to the same governorate batch onto one courier.
Express.d5t: Dispatch hours and the morning wave
Express.d5d: Outside the dispatch window nothing is offered — night orders queue and offer clocks pause, then the first sweep after opening dispatches everything that piled up.
Express.d6d: A courier who accepted or claimed a parcel but never scanned it within the pickup deadline loses the job automatically. Every answered offer feeds a per-courier acceptance rate.
Express.pointsTitle: Hezalli Points — partner shops, not staff
Express.pointsSubtitle: A shop owner registers as a drop-off and hand-off hub. The seller drops the parcel there, the courier collects it, and returns flow back through it too.
Express.shotPointScan: The point's scan station — receive, hand off to courier, or return
Express.shotPointParcels: Parcels currently in the point's custody
Express.p1d: Every handover — seller→point, point→courier, courier→buyer — is proven with a QR scan. Exactly one party is accountable for the parcel at any moment.
Express.p2d: The point earns a handling fee for every parcel that passes through and is delivered, accrued in its own ledger and paid out periodically. Cash stays with the courier.
Express.onboardSubtitle: Modeled on how large fleets onboard: apply, review, activate. Access is never self-granted.
Express.o1d: Name, phone, governorate, and vehicle. Files a pending request — grants nothing.
Express.o2d: Approve or reject with a note. Every decision is audited.
Express.o3d: Approval grants the courier role — their approved vehicle shapes what parcels they can carry.
Express.trackTitle: Everyone can see where it is
Express.tr1d: A public page, also reachable by scanning the label's QR: an Express badge, the estimated delivery, and a full status timeline.
Express.tr2d: Every in-flight parcel, its courier, and its deadline state. Due-by = shipped + the tier's max ETA; overdue parcels sort to the top.
Express.depthTitle: The parts that make it a real operation
Express.depthSubtitle: Delivery is never just "drop it off, done." These close the gaps a live fleet actually hits.
Express.g1d: Recipient name, a doorstep photo, and an optional delivery code — evidence for "never arrived" and COD disputes.
Express.g2d: A per-courier ledger: cash collected minus remitted equals cash on hand, and delivery fees accrue for admins to pay out. A PIN secures it.
Express.g3d: Buyers rate the courier 1–5 stars after each delivery; the average shows on the fleet view alongside their offer-acceptance rate.
Express.g4d: Buyers pin their address and watch the courier approach; couriers get a push the moment a new offer lands, even with the app closed.
Express.g5d: Ops staff hand a whole governorate of unassigned parcels to one courier in a single action — no more one-at-a-time clicking.
Express.g6d: A dedicated ops role: tune vehicle capacities and per-category shipping defaults live, with no redeploy.
Express.c1d: Master switch. Off = only Standard shows at checkout.
Express.c2d: Express price when a store hasn't set its own per-zone fee.
Express.c3d: What a courier earns per completed delivery.
Express.c5d: Minutes a courier has to accept before it cascades onward.
Express.c6d: How many couriers to try before escalating to manual dispatch.
Express.c7d: The dispatch window, Yemen local time; outside it parcels queue.
Express.c8d: Post unassigned parcels on a board couriers can claim from.
Express.c9d: In-flight job cap past which a courier can't claim more.
Express.c10d: Turns on routing parcels through Hezalli Points.
Express.c11d: What a point earns per parcel it routes and sees delivered.
Express.c12d: Failed attempts before a parcel auto-returns to the seller.
Express.roadmapSubtitle: Honest about what's still missing — and what's next.
Express.r1d: Buyers can only pin their address via the browser's current-location lookup today. A freely-draggable map with reverse geocoding is the next upgrade for precise routing.
Express.r2d: Every courier applies and is approved individually today. A fleet-partner tier would let a delivery company onboard once and run its own drivers underneath.
Express.r3d: The map refreshes every 20 seconds today rather than streaming continuous GPS — a step toward tighter real-time tracking during delivery.
Express.r4d: The Web Push plumbing already ships in the code but stays silent until VAPID keys are set in the server environment.
Express.footerNote: Hezalli Express — a full operator overview
